# unch v0.1.1

`v0.1.1` is the last stable annotation-based release before the Tree-sitter transition. It keeps the original `@search:` and `@filectx:` model, but adds the remote and CI flow needed to publish, reuse, and sync repository indexes across machines.

## Highlights

- introduces the reworked CLI surface around `init`, `index`, `search`, `create ci`, `bind ci`, and `remote sync`
- adds `unch create ci` to scaffold the `searcher` GitHub Actions workflow for building and publishing a repository index
- adds remote manifest binding and sync so local checkouts can pull published CI snapshots instead of rebuilding from scratch every time
- publishes `.semsearch/index.db` and `manifest.json` from CI to `gh-pages`
- keeps published index versions stable with logical index hashing in the manifest rather than raw SQLite file hashes
- stores repository-relative paths in the index instead of runner-local absolute paths and hardens CI runtime setup by building `unch` from source with pinned yzma settings

## Upgrade notes

- indexing is still annotation-based in this release: searchable notes come from explicit `@search:` and `@filectx:` comments
- remote search works through the generated `searcher` workflow plus a local `.semsearch/manifest.json` binding created by `unch bind ci <repo-or-workflow-url>`
- the first remote setup is: generate the workflow, commit it, run it once so CI publishes the initial snapshot, then bind the local checkout
- the default runtime may still download the embedding model and yzma libraries on first use
